Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest Defendant Helen Shaws Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Continued To Apr-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Veronica Philipp, Esq. of MURO & LAMPE, INC., T:916.817.6300, for defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
Pavel Krepkiy, Esq. of ANNA DUBROVSKY LAW GROUP, INC., T:415.746.1477 ext.219, for plaintiff;
Nairi Paterson, Esq. of HARTSUYKER, STRATMAN & WILLIAMS-ABREGO, T:510.305.2177, specially appearing for deceased defendant.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and discussed. The Court denies Ms. Paterson's request that the deceased defendant be dismissed.

After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT NRT WEST, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER RESIDENTIAL's Motion To Contest Defendant Helen Shaw's Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement, modified to replace "financial condition" with "estate," as follows:

Defendant NRT West, Inc.'s motion to contest good faith settlement is continued to April 6, 2020. Plaintiff needs to substitute Ms. Shaw's estate into this case. NRT has leave to conduct reasonable discovery regarding Ms. Shaw's financial condition so the court can evaluate whether the settlement is in good faith. (See City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Crt. (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1265.) Any supplemental opposition to be filed and served by March 20, 2020. Any reply to be filed and served by March 27, 2020.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
